title: My Stepdaughter Faked A Gift Letter, Emptied Our $290K Wine Cellar. So I Drained Her Trust Fund
description: When my husband died, his $290,000 Bordeaux wine collection was supposed to pass to me, but my stepdaughter produced a fake notarized gift letter, cleared the cellar, and sold every bottle at auction. I proved the notary stamp was fabricated, won a $310,000 civil fraud judgment, and when she refused to pay I garnished her six-figure trust fund until the debt was satisfied. I then donated the final $20,000 to an anti-forgery education program in her name. #RevengeStory #WineFraud #TrustFundJustice
thumb_text: I GARNISHED HER TRUST FUND AND DONATED IT
pinned_comment: What would you do if your stepdaughter faked a notarized letter and emptied your late husband’s $290K Bordeaux cellar? I garnished her trust fund, but what’s your move? Share your revenge or similar experience in the comments.

thumb_lines.text: I FOUND HIS HANDWRITTEN NOTE BEHIND A LOOSE BRICK
